rulings.Case Title: ALICE BOTTINO et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Extend Site Closure Deadline
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 10. Defendant'S Motion To Extend Site Closure Deadline; GRANTED. Deadline is extended to April 13, 2016. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: H/S PARTNERS II, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. RAYMOND G. CHOY, HUSBAND AS COMMUNITY PROPERTY et al (CCP. 170.6 WONG)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint In Intervention For Declaratory Relief, For Specific Performance, And Damages
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 2. INTERVENOR JOHN WAI Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint In Intervention For Declaratory Relief, For Specific Performance, And Damages; GRANTED. No opposition filed. First Amended Complaint in intervention shall be filed and served forthwith. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STAPLETON, DAVID P., THE COURT-APPOINTED RECEIVER VS. 3066 MARKET STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Af Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 3. PLAINTIFF STAPLETON, DAVID P., THE COURT-APPOINTED RECEIVER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Af Attachment; DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show that attachment is warranted in light of CC 2924k protections. No notice provided to foreclosure trustee. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL COLES et al VS. MARK ONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 11( part 1 of 2). DEFENDANT MARK ONG, RAE CHENG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT; OVERRULED. The Complaint contains sufficient facts to support the First Cause of Action for Violation of San Francisco Administrative Code §37.9(a). See Complaint paragraphs 6-8 . The Complaint also contains sufficient facts to support the Second Cause of Action for Fraud. See Complaint paragraphs 6-8, 23-25. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL COLES et al VS. MARK ONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 11 (part 2 of 2). DEFENDANT MARK ONG, RAE CHENG MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT; DENIED. Facts supporting request for punitive damages are sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TODD V. MAGALINE, AS TRUSTEE OF THE MAGALINE VS. PATRICIA ALLISON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition For Relief From Forfeiture
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 4. DEFENDANT PATRICIA ALLISON Notice Of Petition For Relief From Forfeiture; Continued to April 2., 2014 per ex-parte order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALFRED LEE VS. SEAN MORRIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 5. DEFENDANT SEAN MORRIS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees; GRANTED. Defendant is the prevailing party in an action on a contract. Unlawful Detainer action is based on landlord-tenant relationship arising out of contract. Defendant is entitled to $8,562.50 in fees and $725.00 in costs. The fees are reasonable given the complex legal issue involved in summary judgment. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GAEHWILER SENIOR, TRUSTEE OF THE GAEHWILER et al VS. MARIA MERCADO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall The Writ Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 25, 2014, Line 7. DEFENDANT MARIA MERCADO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall The Writ Of Possession; DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. = (501/REQ)
